title: 06 — Make dependency and toolchain maintenance deterministic
objective: Prevent future drift between package metadata, runtime configuration, Codex, GitHub Actions, deployment tooling, and supported dependency versions.
userContext: Maintainers need routine dependency and toolchain maintenance to be reviewable, grouped by compatibility domain, compatible with trusted auto-merge protections, and supported by deterministic validation or reporting instead of ad hoc manual checks.
currentBehaviour: Dependency update grouping, outdated-package visibility, runtime-version consistency, action pinning comments, and package-manager consistency can drift unless maintainers remember to check multiple files and workflows manually.
requiredBehaviour: Dependabot configuration, repository validation, optional reporting workflows, and documentation must make toolchain and dependency drift visible while keeping major upgrades intentional and routine reports non-noisy.
userExperience: Maintainers receive sensible grouped dependency pull requests, can initiate major upgrades intentionally, can run or inspect deterministic drift reports, and understand how exceptions are recorded and verified.
functionalRequirements: Group updates by compatibility domain, including Next.js, React, and OpenNext; ESLint, TypeScript, and type definitions; Tailwind and PostCSS; Cloudflare tooling; and GitHub Actions.
Keep major-version upgrades intentional and separately reviewable.
Allow sensible grouped minor and patch maintenance.
Ensure dependency pull requests do not bypass sensitive-file or trusted-auto-merge protections.
Add deterministic repository validation or reporting for outdated direct dependencies.
Add deterministic repository validation or reporting for unsupported runtime versions.
Add deterministic repository validation or reporting for disagreement between canonical toolchain sources.
Add deterministic repository validation or reporting for deprecated direct packages.
Add deterministic repository validation or reporting for lockfile/package-manager inconsistency.
Add deterministic repository validation or reporting for stale GitHub Action release comments or unpinned action references.
Where appropriate, add a scheduled manual-reporting workflow with one clear responsibility, explicit permissions, no automatic repository modification, and a useful GitHub Actions summary.
Avoid creating noisy issues or commits for routine reports.
Document the supported Node.js and npm baseline, dependency update grouping, how major upgrades are initiated, how exceptions are recorded, and how maintainers verify the repository is current.
technicalConsiderations: This feature depends on Features 01 through 05 so validation and grouping reflect the final supported runtime and dependency stack.
Do not introduce automatic commits or noisy issue creation for routine reports.
Any scheduled reporting workflow must have explicit least-privilege permissions and must not modify repository contents.
Validation should be deterministic enough for CI and local developer use, with clear failure messages or summary output.
The maintenance model must remain compatible with trusted auto-merge and sensitive-file classification.
Group boundaries should reflect compatibility domains rather than arbitrary package names when possible.
outOfScope: Performing another dependency upgrade sweep beyond what is needed to validate or demonstrate the maintenance model.
Weakening trusted auto-merge, sensitive-file classification, action pinning, or workflow permission controls.
Creating automated repository-modifying bots beyond existing Dependabot behaviour.
Creating noisy scheduled issues for routine reports.
Changing product functionality or application UI.
acceptanceCriteria: Dependabot configuration groups updates by the requested compatibility domains.
Major-version upgrades remain intentional and separately reviewable.
Minor and patch maintenance can be grouped sensibly where compatibility domains allow it.
Dependency pull requests remain subject to sensitive-file and trusted-auto-merge protections.

Deterministic validation or reporting covers outdated direct dependencies, unsupported runtime versions, toolchain source disagreement, deprecated direct packages, lockfile/package-manager inconsistency, stale GitHub Action release comments, and unpinned action references.
Any scheduled reporting workflow has one clear responsibility, explicit least-privilege permissions, no automatic repository modification, and a useful GitHub Actions summary.
Routine reports do not create noisy issues or commits.
Documentation explains the Node.js and npm baseline, dependency grouping, major-upgrade initiation, exception recording, and currentness verification.
The maintenance model is compatible with the trusted auto-merge and sensitive-file classification model.
codexGuidance: Implement after DEV-005. Focus on deterministic maintenance controls, reporting, and documentation rather than performing another broad upgrade.
